Join the Movement

"Can this really work?"

In 2007, I stood on a five-acre plot in Senga village. The land was bare, the soil looked exhausted, and only one old mutuba tree stood tall. Still, my desire for farmland outweighed my doubt.

The Beginning: Bare Ground

When I bought the land, the soils were tired and overworked, and most of the trees had long been cleared. The road was rough murram, riddled with potholes, and so narrow in places that even a small car would struggle to pass.

eco

The Quiet Decision

After early farming losses, I allowed nearly an acre to regenerate naturally, leaving it untouched since 2009. Slowly, life returned, and the space developed its own living rhythm.

Natural Regeneration Patience

To Balikuddembe Forest

Today, that same land has become a mini forest, thriving crops, insects, birds, and animals. What began as a risky decision has slowly become a living laboratory.

Interactive Hillside Map

Explore the working demonstrations that make up our sustainable living hub. Click the hotspots to learn more.

touch_app

Explore the Map

Click on any glowing marker to reveal details about our practical demonstrations.

Zone 1

Community Tree Nursery

The birthplace of the Tree Billionaires Generation. Here, we propagate indigenous and fruit trees for distribution across the community, ensuring biodiversity.

Capacity 50,000 Seedlings
Zone 2

Vermicomposting Bays

Our soil engine. Organic waste is processed by earthworms into rich, biological fertilizer, vital for transforming our compacted clay hillside into rich loam.

Production 2 Tons / Month
water_drop
Zone 3

Water Catchment System

Every drop counts. A network of swales and tanks captures rainwater, preventing runoff and ensuring our nursery survives the dry seasons.

Storage 30,000 Liters
Community Rooted

The Balikuddembe Forest

The community gave the forest a name: Balikuddembe Forest. Balikuddembe loosely translates to "they are at peace." It has become a place of quiet balance between people and nature, a shared resource, and a living classroom.

format_quote
"This shamba will be a paradise — created by you."
Bart Hilhorst

About Us

Rooted in Tradition, Growing for the Future. The Bill of Senga is a sustainable living hub where indigenous knowledge meets modern agricultural science to build thriving ecosystems.

f X photo_camera in
